rationale: Statistics & Data Science had to be kept separate from Mathematics at level 1 (most-contested call in the level-1 review) and from CS & AI. At level 2, it has its own community organization: theoretical statisticians publish in Annals of Statistics, applied statisticians publish in Biometrika and JASA, ML-flavored statisticians publish at NeurIPS, and data-science practitioners publish across all of it. Twelve subspecialties split by methodology type. I made Causal Inference its own card despite its growth being recent — it's now the most distinct methodological identity in the field. I kept Bayesian Methods as its own card too because the Bayesians self-identify hard.

subspecialties.name: Theoretical Statistics
covers: theoretical statistics, decision theory, asymptotic theory, high-dimensional statistics theory, minimax theory, statistical learning theory
Ann Stat, J Royal Stat Soc B

subspecialties.name: Applied Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Regression, mixed models, design of experiments.
covers: regression analysis, mixed and multilevel models, design of experiments, response surface, survey methods, sampling theory
JASA Applied, Biometrika applied

subspecialties.name: Causal Inference
subspecialties.tagline: Potential outcomes, DAGs, IV, RDD, sensitivity analysis.
covers: potential outcomes framework, DAGs, propensity scores, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, sensitivity analysis, causal ML
JCI, Stat Sci causal, AJE methods

subspecialties.name: Bayesian Statistics & Probabilistic Modeling
subspecialties.tagline: Hierarchical models, MCMC, variational, probabilistic programming.
covers: hierarchical Bayesian models, MCMC methodology, variational inference, probabilistic programming, Bayesian nonparametrics, BFFs
Bayesian Analysis, Stan/PyMC research
explicitly_excludes: pure deep generative → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Time Series & Spatial Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Forecasting, state space, point processes, spatial models.
covers: time series methodology, state space models, point processes, spatial and spatiotemporal models, forecasting methodology
J Time Ser Anal, Spatial Stat

subspecialties.name: Statistical Machine Learning
covers: regularization methods, conformal prediction, kernel methods (stats angle), ensemble methods, model selection, statistical learning at the ML/stats border
explicitly_excludes: deep learning methodology → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: High-Dimensional & Sparse Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Lasso, sparse regression, low-rank, compressed sensing.
covers: sparse regression, lasso and variants, low-rank methods, compressed sensing, matrix completion, factor models
EJS, ESAIM Probab Stat
explicitly_excludes: theory of these methods → Theoretical Statistics

subspecialties.name: Experimental Design & Causal A/B Testing
subspecialties.tagline: A/B testing, RCT design, sequential, multi-armed.
covers: randomized experiment design, multi-armed bandits, sequential testing, A/B testing methodology, adaptive trials methodology (statistical angle), reinforcement learning bandits

subspecialties.name: Data Science & Statistical Practice
subspecialties.tagline: Data engineering for stats, reproducibility, methods comparison.
covers: data-science workflows, statistical software, reproducibility research, methods comparison, applied data-science case studies, dashboards-as-stats
Harvard Data Sci Rev

subspecialties.name: Statistical Genomics & Computational Biology Methods
subspecialties.tagline: Methods for single-cell, GWAS, multi-omics integration.
covers: statistical methods for genomics, GWAS methodology, single-cell statistical methods, multi-omics integration methodology, polygenic risk scores

subspecialties.name: Statistical Image & Signal Processing
subspecialties.tagline: Image stats, signal models, computational stats imaging.
covers: statistical image analysis, signal models, statistical imaging methodology, inverse problems methodology
SIAM J Imaging Sci, JMIV
explicitly_excludes: computer vision methods → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Statistical Ethics, Privacy & Fairness
subspecialties.tagline: DP, algorithmic fairness from stats angle, sensitive data methods.
covers: differential privacy theory, statistical fairness criteria and methods, methods for sensitive-data analysis, missing data with sensitivity, replication in stats
explicitly_excludes: broader algorithmic ethics → Computer Science & AI (FAccT)

uncovered_concerns: Considered Operations Research as its own card — moved to Mathematics (Optimization & OR).
Considered Forecasting separately — folded under Time Series.
Considered Statistics Education — small; folds under Social Sciences (Education).
